title: nolina bigelovii subsp. parryi
intro: Nolina bigelovii subsp. parryi, also known as Parry's Bear Grass or Parry's Beargrass, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the Asparagaceae family, specifically the genus Nolina. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and treating wounds, as well as potentially aiding in the management of diabetes and other conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
